title: Soy Sauce on Cotton
stainChemistry: Soy sauce is a brown-black stain of melanoidins (caramel-type compounds from fermentation), tannin-like phenolics, amino acids, and — critically — salt. The melanoidins bond to cotton through hydrogen bonding AND some Maillard-type cross-linking. The salt content acts as a MORDANT — a dye fixative that locks the brown color deeper into the fiber the longer it sits. This makes soy sauce genuinely time-sensitive: the same spill caught in 5 minutes is twice as easy as the same spill at 30 minutes. Cotton is pure cellulose — tolerates acid-side spotters, NSD, 3% H₂O₂, and hot wash.
whyThisWorks: Cold flush removes the salt before it can finish mordanting the color into the fiber. NSD suspends loose pigment. Acid-side tannin spotter disrupts the melanoidin-cellulose hydrogen bonds. 3% H₂O₂ oxidizes the residual brown chromophore. Hot wash finishes. The salt-flush first is the difference between soy-sauce-as-removable-tannin and soy-sauce-as-dye-fixed-by-its-own-salt.
defaultAssumption: Time is the main variable. Fresh (within 10 min) is a high-confidence DIY. Hour-old adds a peroxide cycle. Overnight has set most of the way — expect a plateau at faint brown without pro chemistry.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. This step is doing TWO jobs: moving soluble pigment AND flushing out the salt that would otherwise mordant the color into the fiber. Flush longer than you would for a straight tannin — 60+ seconds. The longer the salt sits, the harder the stain fixes.
Apply NSD, sponge gently. Suspends residual pigment. Blot with a clean towel.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on the brown residue. Disrupts the melanoidin-cellulose bonds. Dwell 3–5 min, blot. Repeat once if needed.
Residual brown: 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop. Oxidizes the melanoidin chromophore. Dwell, blot. One oxidizer at a time.
Rinse until water runs clear. Launder hot per care label.

steps: 1. Cold flush from the back — longer than a standard tannin. Salt removal is job #1.
2. NSD in cold water. Sponge, 2–3 min.
3. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A). 3–5 min.
4. 3% H₂O₂ on residual brown. 3–5 min. One pass.
5. NSD flush, hot wash per care label.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Cold flush first AND longer — the salt mordants the color if it sits
One oxidizer at a time, NSD-flushed between

homeSolutions.instruction: Soy sauce is different from most stains: it has a lot of salt, and the salt LOCKS the brown color into cotton the longer it sits. The more seconds tick by, the harder it fixes. Move fast. If you can get cold water on it in the first 30 seconds, you're in great shape.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water, Back of Fabric, Longer Than Usual
homeSolutions.instruction: Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water for at least a full minute — longer than you'd use on most tannin stains. You're flushing BOTH the pigment AND the salt out. The salt is what would otherwise fix the stain permanently.
Work a drop of Dawn dish soap into the stain with a fingertip or soft toothbrush. Let it sit 5 minutes. Rinse cool.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar Dab for Residual Brown
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 teaspoon white vinegar with 4 tablespoons cool water. Dab onto the remaining stain, let sit 3 minutes, blot. The vinegar's mild acidity breaks the melanoidin bond.
If brown remains: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide on the spot, let it bubble 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark or dyed cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia — toxic fume risk.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder on the hottest setting the care label allows. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — dryer heat sets any residual brown permanently.

If the soy sauce sat for hours before treatment, the salt has already done its job and home chemistry may plateau at a faint brown. Take it to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Soy sauce on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% hydrogen peroxide."

steps: 1. Move fast — salt in soy sauce mordants the color.
2. Cold water flush from the BACK for 60+ seconds (longer than regular tannin).
3. Dawn + cold water, 5 min dwell, rinse.
4. Diluted white vinegar dab for residual brown.
5. 3% hydrogen peroxide on stubborn shadow. Skip on dark cotton.
6. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if soy sauce sat for hours before treatment and home chemistry plateaus at a faint brown shadow. Take it to a dry cleaner.
warnings: Speed matters — salt fixes the color
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never let soy sauce sit without a flush — the salt will mordant the color in
Never layer oxidizers — one at a time
Never dry with residual color visible
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash
Dyed cotton: skip or inside-seam-test peroxide

materialWarnings: Flush salt fast — it fixes the color
Cold during spotting, hot at wash
Peroxide on whites; test inside seam on dark cotton

escalation.when: Soy sauce sat for hours before treatment, heat-set by iron or dryer, or two home rounds plateau at faint brown.
escalation.whatToTell: "Soy sauce on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂. [Hot-washed / not yet laundered.]"

contextQuestions: How long ago did the spill happen?
Has the fabric been through any wash cycle yet?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
Light or dark soy sauce? (dark / mushroom / tamari has more melanoidin — harder)
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The salt-mordant mechanism is the novel feature. A minute of extra cold flush time at the start of treatment saves two peroxide cycles later.
